topic: New Jerusalem pole axis — c5 post EXP-174 integration (asymmetric-unfalsifiability defense neutralization)
change_summary: After c4 (2026-04-17T19:42), EXP-174 was integrated (closed ISS-1084 at 2026-04-17T20:23). detail_verdict_text grew from 1,341 → 3,025 chars (+125%) by inserting a new middle paragraph that explicitly names and refutes the asymmetric-unfalsifiability defense (the "two meanings of CONFIRMED" gambit). The original first and third paragraphs are unchanged; detail_evidence is unchanged; tldr fields unchanged; verdict unchanged (Unfalsifiable).
our_argument_summary: Three-paragraph verdict: (1) original scorecard-inflation framing (Eschatological prophecy mapped to axis, no falsification criteria, pole-axis absent from Revelation 21, dome monitoring excludes it); (2) NEW anti-asymmetry paragraph (names the dome-defender move that we are applying unfalsifiability asymmetrically, concedes kernel of truth about currently-untestable mainstream extrapolations, refutes the comparison by arguing physicists do not label untestable consequences CONFIRMED and tally them with measured quantities, names the category error, offers the fix: single definition of CONFIRMED or separate scorecards); (3) original post-hoc paragraph. The integration explicitly addresses the c3 defense_survives=3 hole and the c4 residual major finding.

tldr_review.notes: tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict unchanged from c4. Both remain factually accurate summaries of detail_evidence (theological mapping, no measurable consequence, no monitoring test). TLDR does not need to carry the new anti-asymmetry argument — it operates at the right level of compression for a non-technical reader and does not mislead.

exp_174_asymmetry_neutralization.notes: The c4 preemptive_recommendation suggested a ~3-sentence insertion naming the "two definitions of CONFIRMED" move. The integration expands this to a substantive ~1,700-char paragraph that (a) names the advocate move, (b) concedes kernel of truth, (c) offers the black-hole-interior and string-vacua comparisons, (d) distinguishes THEORETICAL CONSEQUENCE (not labeled CONFIRMED, reported separately) from EMPIRICAL CONFIRMATION (measurement-matched-to-threshold), (e) names the category error, (f) offers the fix. Substance matches the c4 recommendation; delivery is longer and more rhetorically developed than the minimum request. Acceptable — the expanded treatment is stronger than the minimal version.

c4_minor_holes_status.hole_1_hardcoded_4_theological: UNFIXED. detail_evidence still hardcodes "4 theological claims in the current WIN count (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034)." The c4 recommendation to replace with a build-time count or anchored tally was not applied.
c4_minor_holes_status.hole_2_two_independent_layers: UNFIXED. detail_evidence still says "unfalsifiable on two independent layers — the dome geometry and the prophetic framework." The c4 recommendation to replace with "the prophetic framework ... and the pole-axis mapping" was not applied; the current phrasing still concedes that the dome geometry is itself unfalsifiable, which is in tension with the rest of the review.
c4_minor_holes_status.hole_3_geopolitical_context_html: UNFIXED. detail_verdict_text still says "the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is theological-political assertion, not testable physics" with no anchor or quote. The c4 recommendation was to cite a specific sentence or drop the clause.
c4_minor_holes_status.hole_4_monitor_py_duplicate: UNFIXED. detail_evidence still contains two separate mentions of monitor.py excluding WIN-032 (once in the early sentences about the monitoring audit engine, again in the later sentence about validation domain). The c4 recommendation was to merge or delete the second instance.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt (live dashboard domain enumeration — no WIN-032 domain)
raw-text/02-wins.txt (line 117: "WIN-032 — New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED")

data/sections.json (Part 3.1b = id="posthoc-standard" — content is about post-hoc vs prospective temporal standard, NOT scorecard inflation; Part 9.1 contains Pattern 6 = "Inconvenient Data Discarded")
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — WIN-032 has no code. The dome treats it as a categorical theological assertion; our review does not attempt to derive any quantity from it. The only verifiable claims are bibliographic (Revelation 21), site-structural (monitor.py absence), and cross-WIN consistency (the four Unfalsifiable WINs).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The "4 theological claims" figure in detail_evidence is hardcoded and not computed at build time. This violates the project's own "never hardcode a number derivable from data" rule and is the c4 minor hole that persists.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: N/A — WIN-032 has no numerical prediction; the "escape" is definitional ("CONFIRMED means consistent with dome geometry"). The new EXP-174 paragraph now explicitly refuses this escape by insisting on one definition of CONFIRMED across the scorecard.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. All monitor.py-absence claims verified by direct grep.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: grep -i "032|jerusalem" on raw-text/monitor.py and raw-text/08-live-power.txt returns zero matches. monitor.py has no domain, no fetch, and no comparison logic for WIN-032. Current tag in wins.json: monitoring=none. Correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: This is a theological assertion about a pole-axis mapping. Standard physics does not make any prediction about the descent location of the New Jerusalem (it does not treat the New Jerusalem as a physical object at all). The dome model is not renaming a standard mechanism here; it is asserting a theological geometric claim with no mainstream analog. Tag relabels_standard=false is correct.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The biblical text of Revelation 21 predates the dome model by roughly 1,930 years. The claim "the New Jerusalem descends along the dome pole axis" takes a known text and maps it onto dome geometry after the dome geometry was chosen. Under our stricter post-hoc standard (Part 3.1b), the claim fails both (a) temporal impossibility and (b) provable unawareness. The c4 verification of post_hoc=true via date analysis is correct.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The claim does not follow from dome geometric equations. It is a stipulated mapping: the author has chosen to identify the pole axis (a dome geometric feature) with the descent axis of the New Jerusalem (a textual feature). No equation produces "pole axis" from "New Jerusalem." Tag derives_from_dome=false is correct.

holes_found.description: Broken cross-reference in the new EXP-174 paragraph. The new detail_verdict_text concludes: "...the fix is not to relitigate the theology but to insist on a single definition of confirmation — which is what our Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6) already does." Neither name exists at that location. Part 3.1b (id=posthoc-standard) is "Post-hoc vs Prospective — Our Standard vs the Author's" — a temporal-standard argument, not scorecard inflation. Pattern 6 in Part 9.1 is "Inconvenient Data Discarded" (StarWalk H=4750 "untrusted," failed predictions "suspended," sun altitude as "optical illusion"), not mixing theological and empirical claims in one scorecard. A dome defender following the anchor finds a different argument; a careful reader concludes we invented a name for an argument we did not make. The strongest relevant argument (mixing empirical and theological claims in one scorecard) is made scattered across Part 2 ("expand the scorecard into new domains"), Part 6 ("asymmetric scorecard pattern"), and in the new WIN-032 paragraph itself — there is no single named place called "Scorecard-Inflation (Pattern 6)."
holes_found.recommendation: Fix the reference. Options: (a) drop the specific named citation — the paragraph stands on its own without needing to point to "Part 3.1b, Pattern 6"; (b) point to the actually-relevant section (Part 2's V51.0 scorecard-expansion argument, or Part 6's asymmetric scorecard-pattern section); (c) add a new named anchor (id="scorecard-inflation") to one of those sections and rename the paragraph here to match. Option (a) is safest and lowest-risk. If option (b) is chosen, prefer pointing to Part 6 (accuracy denominator discussion) since it is already about the denominator-mixing problem.

holes_found.description: c4 hole #1 persists: detail_evidence still hardcodes "4 theological claims in the current WIN count (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034)." Violates the "never hardcode a count derivable from data" rule. If a fifth Unfalsifiable WIN is added (or one of the four is re-classified), the prose silently goes stale.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with a build-time template ({{UNFALSIFIABLE_WIN_COUNT}} theological claims) or a rendering hook that enumerates Unfalsifiable-verdict WINs from wins.json. Carried forward from c4 holes_found[1].

holes_found.description: c4 hole #2 persists: detail_evidence phrasing "unfalsifiable on two independent layers — the dome geometry and the prophetic framework" still concedes that the dome geometry is itself unfalsifiable. This is in tension with the rest of our review, which argues the dome geometry IS falsifiable (and falsified) on multiple grounds.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with: "unfalsifiable on two independent layers — the prophetic framework (no measurable consequence, no timeline) and the pole-axis mapping (not present in the source text, added by the dome author)." Carried forward from c4 holes_found[3].

holes_found.description: c4 hole #3 persists: detail_verdict_text still says "the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is theological-political assertion, not testable physics" with no quote, no anchor, no specific passage. A dome defender can and will ask which framing, what page, what sentence.
holes_found.recommendation: Either cite a specific sentence from raw-text/07-ai-context.txt with anchor, or drop the clause — the theological/unfalsifiability basis is already sufficient. Carried forward from c4 holes_found[4].

holes_found.description: c4 hole #4 persists: internal duplication inside detail_evidence — monitor.py-excludes-WIN-032 is stated twice (once early: "The dome's own automated monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include WIN-032..."; once late: "The model's own monitoring system (monitor.py) contains no validation domain for WIN-032..."). The two sentences make the same argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Delete the second sentence or merge. Carried forward from c4 holes_found[2].

holes_found.description: In the new EXP-174 paragraph, the example "pre-CMB cosmology, black-hole interiors, or string-theoretic vacua, all of which are currently untestable" bundles three items with very different scientific statuses. Pre-CMB cosmology has measurable signatures (e.g., baryon asymmetry, primordial gravitational wave constraints); black-hole interiors are deterministic consequences of GR with no external handle; string-theoretic vacua are a theory-space problem. A physicist-defender could challenge the example grouping as imprecise. This does not break the argument (the structural claim about scorecard mixing stands), but the grouping is weaker than it needs to be.
holes_found.recommendation: Either narrow to the cleanest example (black-hole interiors — they are the only one that is both a derived consequence AND genuinely inaccessible to observation) or prefix with "examples of varying strength — e.g., black-hole interiors, string-theoretic vacua, certain inflation parameter ranges." Minor rhetorical improvement; argument survives without the fix.

stronger_arguments.description: Strengthen the scorecard-mixing argument by quantifying the dilution. The dome quotes an accuracy percentage (e.g., 94.7%, 96.3%, 100.0% depending on denominator — see Part 6). Removing the four Unfalsifiable WINs from both numerator and denominator should change the percentage. If the change is small, the dome has mostly-measurable WINs and the theological inclusion is a minor category error; if the change is large, the theological WINs are materially inflating accuracy. Even without the EXP-174 paragraph, this is a cleaner numerical argument than the current qualitative framing.

The newly-integrated asymmetric-unfalsifiability argument would be reinforced by a direct quote from a physics textbook or review paper showing that theoretical consequences are reported separately from confirmed predictions. E.g., PDG (Particle Data Group) reviews separate "measured values" from "theoretical constraints"; NIST physical constants are listed as measurements with uncertainties rather than as "confirmed predictions of the Standard Model." One explicit citation here would pre-empt the "you are just asserting mainstream practice" rebuttal.
sources: Particle Data Group Review of Particle Physics (distinguishes measured from theoretical values)
NIST CODATA physical constants report structure

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model is correct that mainstream physics contains currently-untestable extrapolations (black-hole interiors, pre-recombination cosmology, high-energy vacuum structure). A consistent methodology that excludes WIN-032 as unfalsifiable has to explain why those mainstream extrapolations are treated differently. The new EXP-174 paragraph now does exactly this explanation work, and the paragraph correctly distinguishes theoretical consequence from empirical confirmation.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel-of-truth is about methodological symmetry, not about the specific claim. Even if mainstream physics sometimes mixes testable and non-testable claims in ways we would also criticize, WIN-032's specific problem is that it is categorized as CONFIRMED and counted alongside measured quantities without any pretense of measurement. The dome author has the option to relabel WIN-032 as a theological-framework claim rather than a confirmed prediction; instead he has chosen the CONFIRMED label, which is the category error our text now names explicitly.

advocate_mode.best_defense: You have now explicitly named and refused the "two definitions of CONFIRMED" move, which was our strongest position. The remaining angle is narrower: even if we accept that mixing CONFIRMED-as-measurement with CONFIRMED-as-theologically-consistent is a category error in the scorecard, you have not shown that WIN-032 is a theological claim rather than a geometric one. The dome model defines the pole axis mathematically (disc_radius, symmetry axis of the ovoid cavity) and we assert that the New Jerusalem descends along THAT specific geometric feature. This is a geometric claim about a symmetry axis — the fact that it is motivated by a biblical text does not make it theological in the scorecard-inflation sense. The analogous mainstream claim would be saying Einstein's GR field equations predict gravitational waves; the prediction is mathematical even if Einstein's motivation was aesthetic.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The defense is thin — it relies on re-framing a scriptural assertion about Revelation 21 as a geometric theorem, which does not survive inspection of the Revelation 21 text (no axis, no pole, no coordinates — confirmed in c4 Lens C). The current prose already addresses this in paragraph 1: "the pole-axis location is not in the source text (Revelation 21) but was added by the dome author." That sentence survives the re-framing attack. No additional text required. The defense collapses at 1.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Unchanged from c4. All four Unfalsifiable WINs continue to share the same adversarial architecture (absence from monitor.py, post-hoc retrofitting of ancient text to dome geometry, scorecard-inflation pattern). The new EXP-174 paragraph in WIN-032 is the most developed treatment — it would be worth back-porting a compressed version (or an anchor reference) into WIN-023, WIN-031, and WIN-034 so the argument is consistently applied across the four. As-is, WIN-032 now carries the full scorecard-mixing neutralization while its three sibling WINs do not, which is an asymmetry in rigor rather than a contradiction. Flag as potential expansion target for analyst Mode 2b (consistency propagation).

claims_checked: "the pole-axis location is not in the source text (Revelation 21) but was added by the dome author" — re-verified against Revelation 21 canonical text (KJV, public domain). No axis, no pole, no coordinates specified. The claim is accurate.

"4 theological claims in the current WIN count (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034)" — re-verified against current data/wins.json: Object.entries(wins.reduce((a,x)=>(a[x.verdict]=(a[x.verdict]||0)+1,a),{})).filter(([v,c])=>v==="Unfalsifiable") returns Unfalsifiable:4, and the four IDs match (023,031,032,034). The count is numerically correct but structurally fragile (hardcoded — see hole #2).
"The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include WIN-032" — re-verified. grep -i "032|jerusalem" on raw-text/monitor.py returns zero matches.
ISS-1084 fix metadata: closed_at 2026-04-17T20:23:10Z, fixed_by expansion-integration-EXP-174 — verified against monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.

c4 text_fingerprint detail_verdict_length=1341 vs current 3025 — drift confirmed. The +1,684 character delta corresponds almost exactly to the new middle paragraph (approximately 1,680 chars by count).
quantitative_verification.computations: Ran verdict tally via node one-liner; grep on monitor.py; direct inspection of Revelation 21; file-stat on EXP-174 integration and closed-issues.json metadata.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None on numerical claims. The single discrepancy is structural (the "Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6)" pointer is wrong — see major hole above), but this is a cross-reference error, not a numerical one.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-174 integration is substantively strong and does the work the c3/c4 reviews requested. The asymmetric-unfalsifiability defense now has a named, in-prose refusal, and the defense_survives rating correctly drops from 2 to 1. However, the integration introduced a new citation/cross-reference bug (Scorecard-Inflation / Part 3.1b, Pattern 6) that a careful dome defender will notice immediately — the named anchor points to the wrong section, and Pattern 6 is something else in Part 9. This is a simple copy-edit fix but it is major because broken cross-references are exactly the kind of "gotcha" a defender seeking to discredit the review will seize on. Meanwhile, all four c4 minor holes remain unfixed — not a blocker, but the backlog is still there. Verdict Unfalsifiable holds; recommended_action is minor_edit (primarily: fix the cross-reference; secondarily: apply the four c4 recommendations).

citations_failed: "our Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6)" — Part 3.1b is id=posthoc-standard and covers post-hoc/prospective standard, not scorecard inflation. Pattern 6 in Part 9.1 is "Inconvenient Data Discarded." Neither location matches the named argument. This is a cross-reference failure introduced by the EXP-174 integration.
citations_unchecked: "pre-CMB cosmology, black-hole interiors, or string-theoretic vacua, all of which are currently untestable" — used as illustrative examples, not cited; not strictly a citation. See minor hole #5 for the rhetorical concern.
